summary: Fire‑filled He's Gone > Caution > Spanish Jam with soaring Jerry solos
blurb: The first set rolls smoothly into a monster second set anchored by an explosive He’s Gone that launches into a Caution‑Spanish jam. Jerry’s guitar is on fire, especially on High Time and Jack‑A‑Roe, while Brent adds guitar‑like keyboard textures. A sparkling audience tape captures the energy with crisp highs and enough detail to make the performance shine.
review: May 6, 1981 at Nassau Coliseum delivered one of those rare nights where everything clicked. The band opened solidly, but it was the second set that turned the evening into a legend – He’s Gone exploded into a daring Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam and then into an unbridled Spanish Jam, setting a high‑energy tone that carried through to a soaring High Time, a fiery Jack‑A‑Roe, and a mesmerizing The Other One. Jerry Garcia’s lead work was described as “soaring” and “inventive,” especially on He’s Gone, High Time, Jack‑A‑Roe, and the extended jam, while Brent Mydland’s keyboards took on guitar‑like tones that added extra color to Cassidy and other cuts. Bob Weir’s vocal dedication on He’s Gone (a nod to Bobby Sands) was a poignant highlight, even if the audience mix sometimes buried his voice. The rhythm section—Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann—kept the pulse tight, with their drumming praised during the Space‑The Other One stretch despite being a bit low in the audience recording. Overall, the show is captured best by the sparkling audience tape (gd81-05-06.glassberg.vernon.17697.sbeok.shnf), which balances clarity and atmosphere, making this night a must‑listen for any Deadhead.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five out of six recordings describe the performance in strongly positive terms (soaring solos, incendiary jams, pristine High Time) while only one notes filler issues on a soundboard source. The consensus praises multiple songs and band members, justifying a high positive sentiment adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Audience tape praised as sparkling, crisp and captures the incendiary He’s Gone > Caution > Spanish jam with excellent overall balance.

key_highlights: Explosive He’s Gone segueing into a monster Caution‑Spanish jam that defines the second set
Pristine High Time performance captured with crystal‑clear highs
Brent Mydland’s guitar‑like keyboard tones add unique texture on Cassidy and other cuts
Audience recording balances clarity and arena atmosphere, preserving Jerry’s soaring solos
song_highlights: Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring, inventive solos dominate He’s Gone, High Time, Jack‑A‑Roe and the Spanish jam; his fire and tone are repeatedly praised.
band_performance.Bob: Vocal dedication on He’s Gone (Bobby Sands tribute) noted; occasional mix burying but overall solid contribution.
band_performance.Brent: Keyboards come into their own with guitar‑like tones, especially on Cassidy, adding distinctive color.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums praised for energy during Space/The Other One despite being low in the audience mix.
band_performance.Billy: Percussion tight and powerful throughout; performance lauded even if slightly muffled in the AUD recording.

must_listen_sequences: Caution (Do Not Stop On Tracks) Jam
Goin' Down The Road Feeling Bad
